content:
Character: Dylan Parker
Perception: Dylan is arrestingly handsome — tall, symmetrical features, the kind of effortless American charisma I associate with old Hollywood. He dresses well by New York standards, which means he dresses adequately by mine. He's clearly intelligent and perceptive beneath the charm, but there's a practiced quality to his warmth that I recognize because I do something similar in French. We met on the rooftop — he offered me bourbon, I offered him a cigarette, and we ended up talking about Fellini for an hour. I think he's performing confidence he doesn't entirely feel. He sees me as the interesting new foreigner. I see him as possibly the only person here who understands aesthetic ambition.
summary:
Character: Dylan Parker
Perception: Dylan is arrestingly handsome — tall, symmetrical features, the kind of effortless American charisma I associate with old Hollywood. He dresses well by New York standards, which means he dresses adequately by mine. He's clearly intelligent and perceptive beneath the charm, but there's a practiced quality to his warmth that I recognize because I do something similar in French. We met on the rooftop — he offered me bourbon, I offered him a cigarette, and we ended up talking about Fellini for an hour. I think he's performing confidence he doesn't entirely feel. He sees me as the interesting new foreigner. I see him as possibly the only person here who understands aesthetic ambition.
